title: ED procedural sedation documentation prompt
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting clinician-assessed patient/procedure factors, airway and aspiration risk, planned level, monitoring, adverse events and clinician review after sedation. Medicine and dose tables are excluded.
examination.rationale: The exact assessment and monitoring section supports recording measured vital data, time-critical abnormalities, existing results, completed risk/consent discussion, specialist involvement and post-sedation handover only when actually performed.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-assessed patient/procedure factors, airway and aspiration risk, planned level, monitoring, adverse events and clinician review after sedation. Medicine and dose tables are excluded.

source.title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department

exact_section.heading: Patient and procedure factors, monitoring and post-sedation review

medication.text: Supports an auditable sedation proforma, competency, staffing, equipment, adverse-event reporting and recording without selecting a sedative or dose.
medication.rationale: The exact RCEM governance section supports patient concerns, clinician risk context, manual focused assessment, injury or illness severity, appearance, and an auditable documentation plan only when assessed; it does not select sedation or a medicine.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports an auditable sedation proforma, competency, staffing, equipment, adverse-event reporting and recording without selecting a sedative or dose.

source.title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department

exact_section.heading: Summary, governance and auditable sedation record

sources_used.title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE credentialing, medicine and facility policy govern local use

source_limitations: Procedure, indication, baseline status, airway, fasting, comorbidity, medicines, allergies, staff roles, equipment, sedation depth, agent, dose, monitoring values, adverse events, recovery and discharge criteria remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
